signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Risk + Carry + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 162.68, holding firmly above its SMA20/50/200 cluster (162.05/160.62/157.46). The pair is underpinned by a +261bp SOIS carry advantage for the USD, while headlines confirm a de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz, driving crude prices higher and supporting USD demand. Despite the bullish spot trend, the 25d risk reversal at -1.40 (Saxo) reveals a moderate put skew, signaling that the options market is aggressively pricing protection against a JPY snapback or BoJ intervention. COT data shows a crowded JPY net short of -105,238 contracts, leaving the pair highly vulnerable to a disorderly unwind if geopolitical tensions trigger a flight to safety. Carry remains the dominant driver while VIX sits at 17.5, but intervention risk rises sharply on any extension beyond current levels. Risk: VIX breaks above 25, activating JPY safe-haven flows and forcing a carry liquidation.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + CPI Catalyst
signals.text: NZD/USD has gathered strength to 0.5860, clearing its SMA20/50/200 cluster (0.5729/0.5804/0.5823) following a CPI inflation beat that bolsters RBNZ hike bets. This hawkish fundamental shift directly contradicts the extreme speculative positioning, where COT LF net shorts sit at -29,582 (only 15.2% long). Retail sentiment is also heavily skewed with 73% short, creating a high-conviction setup for a short-squeeze as specs are forced to cover into the RBNZ's hawkish bias. While the USD maintains a +125bp nominal carry advantage, the NZD real rate of -0.58% is set to tighten if inflation persistence forces the RBNZ to act on its hiking bias. The pair's technical position above all major SMAs confirms the bullish momentum shift. Risk: RBNZ holds and signals a peak in rates, validating the existing spec short.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD trades at 1.1426, hovering just above its SMA20 (1.1416) but remaining capped by the SMA50 at 1.1526. The options market is pricing an elevated vol premium with IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.7%, a +2.5% VRP that suggests significant hedging demand ahead of the ECB meeting on 23 July. This caution is supported by a strong negative Economic Surprise Index (CESI) of -17 for the Eurozone, indicating persistent data misses relative to the US CESI of +48. COT data shows specs are net short EUR (-74,637), aligning with the +140bp SOFR-ESTR carry advantage favoring the USD. The combination of poor data and a restrictive US real rate (+1.47%) suggests EUR/USD upside remains structurally limited. Risk: ECB delivers a hawkish surprise on 23 July, forcing a re-evaluation of the carry deficit.

signals.title: AUD/CHF — Carry + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/CHF is supported by a massive +435bp carry premium (RBA 4.35% vs SNB 0.0%), keeping the cross bid despite a strong negative CESI for AUD (-13). Retail sentiment has reached an extreme 93% short, fading the carry-driven move, which typically serves as a contrarian indicator for further upside. The pair is currently benefiting from a 'normal' volatility regime (VIX 17.5), allowing carry-seeking flows to dominate the SNB's accommodative real rate of -0.62%. However, the AUD/USD technical position above its SMA20 but below its SMA50 suggests the broader AUD recovery is not yet synchronized across all pairs. Carry remains the primary driver for the CHF cross until a shift in global risk appetite occurs. Risk: VIX spikes above 25, triggering a rapid compression of the AUD/CHF carry spread.
